sprint_meta.notes: Agent 09 — 3D visual mapping: billboards vs textures, tile_sprites integration (Round 1, no code).

round_meta.build_target: Specs & contracts only — implementation in later rounds

wk21_r1_kickoff.prompt_text: Spec visual mapping: textured primitives vs pixel-art billboards; how game/graphics/tile_sprites.py feeds ursina_renderer.py; acceptance criteria and risks.

response.raw: Scope clarity: game/graphics/tile_sprites.py is TileSpriteLibrary — procedural pygame Surfaces for terrain categories (grass, water, path, tree) keyed by tile_type and map (x,y) with deterministic variants. It does not contain building or unit sprites; those live elsewhere (e.g. building_sprites.py, hero_sprites.py). WK21 utilization of tile_sprites.py in Ursina is therefore primarily the ENVIRONMENT: a ground layer whose texels match the 2D game’s tile grid. Recommended split: (1) Terrain — per-tile textures sampled from TileSpriteLibrary.get(tile_type, tx, ty, size=TILE_SIZE) mapped onto floor-aligned quads or a batched/atlas approach (perf contract with Agent 03). Tree tiles can render as the same tile texture on a floor quad, or optionally a vertical billboard/crossed planes at tile center for height (Phase 2 polish). (2) Buildings & lairs — hybrid: keep volumetric boxes for silhouette; apply textures via UV-mapped prism (best consistency with 3D camera) using building art surfaces when wired — if only terrain is ready first, interim Y-locked or multi-face billboards from building_sprites renders. Pure camera-facing billboards for large bases read as cardboard and flatten depth; prefer textured boxes once facades exist. (3) Mobile units (heroes, enemies, peasants, guards) — camera-facing billboards (or cylindrically constrained billboards) with pixel frames give the strongest match to existing 32×32 art direction; textured cylinders/cubes fight pixel aspect and animation. Bridge pattern: pygame Surface → PIL Image → Ursina Texture (same family as camera.ui overlay), with aggressive caching of Texture objects per variant/atlas region — never rebuild per entity per frame.
summary_bullets: tile_sprites.py → Ursina: terrain-only contract. Each sim tile (tx, ty) resolves to the same tile_type as the pygame world; TileSpriteLibrary.get(...) produces the albedo for that cell on the X/Z floor.
Environment: prefer a visible ground grid (one quad per tile at y≈0 or a stitched subtexture/atlas) over a single solid color plane — matches plan objective 2 and reuses existing pixel art.

Buildings/lairs: default to textured 3D prisms (existing cube footprint) with facades from building_sprites / future atlas — not from tile_sprites. If textures slip, fallback is crossed billboards or Y-axis-only billboards; avoid full camera billboards for wide footprints.
Units: billboards facing the active camera (Billboard component or manual look-at) using hero/enemy sprite pipeline — orthogonal to tile_sprites.

Performance: cache Surfaces from TileSpriteLibrary (already cached) and cache Ursina Texture handles per discrete variant; avoid 150×150 unique textures per frame — batching/atlas is an architecture decision for Agent 03.
Transparency: tile surfaces use SRCALPHA; ground quads should respect alpha where art uses it; tree tiles may need separate treatment (floor decal vs vertical sprite).
proposed_changes: Define a TerrainTextureBridge API (spec only): inputs (tile_type, tx, ty) → cached Ursina Texture or UV rect in atlas.
Document entity-class visual path: terrain → tile_sprites; buildings → building_sprites UV or billboard; units → billboard sprites.
Add QA visual checklist: side-by-side pygame vs Ursina screenshot at same seed/camera region for terrain parity.
acceptance_criteria: Ground visually matches the 2D game’s terrain vocabulary (grass/water/path/tree) at the per-tile level when comparing the same map region — driven by TileSpriteLibrary, not ad hoc colors.
Buildings remain readable as volumes from the current perspective camera (not paper-thin cards) once textured; lairs distinguishable from player buildings by art or color pass.
Heroes/enemies read as pixel characters (billboard path) or approved interim — not muddy stretched bitmaps on cylinders.
Frame time stays within agreed budget after caching (exact ms threshold per Agent 10/03 — no per-frame full-map texture uploads).
No regression to sim determinism: tile_sprites remains deterministic by (tile_type, x, y); Ursina only displays, never feeds sim.
risks: 150×150 tiles × unique Texture objects = memory and creation spikes — must atlas or tile-chunk LOD.
Pygame → PIL → Ursina path is CPU-heavy if done lazily; startup bake vs incremental streaming risk.
Billboard units can intersect terrain or each other; need Y offset and depth bias/sorting artifacts at shallow angles.
Textured building boxes need consistent UV layout or seams show; authoring burden if done per-building-type.
Tree tiles as flat ground art may look like ‘painted bushes’ in 3D — acceptable MVP; vertical tree representation is extra scope.
Mixing billboard units with perspective can expose empty sides if rotation is wrong — test with camera orbit/pan.
dependencies: Agent 03: texture atlas vs instanced quads, entity budget, and when to rebuild terrain if map mutates.
Read access to world tile grid from GameEngine/game state in Ursina tick — same truth as pygame renderer.
Future: building_sprites.py and hero_sprites surfaces for non-terrain entities; WK21 R1 can spec without implementing.
questions_back_to_pm: Is WK21 strictly terrain + colored primitives until building textures land, or is building UV art in scope for the same milestone?
Target hardware floor for atlas size / texture memory?
recommended_next_actions: Agent 03 reviews terrain batching options against Ursina/Panda3D constraints.
Spike: one chunk of map (e.g. 32×32 tiles) as textured quads + cached textures — measure ms and VRAM.
Round 2: implement TerrainTextureBridge + ground grid only; then buildings; then unit billboards.

visual_strategy.terrain: TileSpriteLibrary → floor quads or atlas; primary use of tile_sprites.py
visual_strategy.buildings_lairs: Textured volumetric prisms preferred; billboards secondary fallback
visual_strategy.units: Camera-facing pixel billboards (hero/enemy art), not tile_sprites

tile_sprites_to_ursina_flow: Sim/world provides (tile_x, tile_y, tile_type) aligned with pygame map.
Surface → Image → Ursina Texture (cached); assign to floor Entity model='quad' per tile or atlas subrect
